  "findings": [
    "Formwork bracing/kickers are inadequate to resist hydrostatic and dynamic concrete pour pressure.",
    "Insufficient lateral support creates risk of form blowout, collapse, or uncontrolled concrete release during placement.",
    "Defective bracing configuration does not meet engineered drawings or ACI 347 requirements for formwork design and lateral load resistance.",
    "Workers in the pour zone are exposed to struck-by/crushing hazards if formwork fails under pressure."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop concrete placement immediately in the affected area.",
    "Prohibit personnel from entering the fall/collapse zone until bracing is corrected.",
    "Have a competent person inspect all formwork bracing, kickers, ties, and walers against the engineered drawings.",
    "Install additional bracing/kickers, ties, and supports as required by the engineer-of-record and ACI 347.",
    "Obtain written approval from the engineer-of-record or qualified design professional before resuming the pour.",
    "Brief the crew on the corrected bracing plan and maintain a safety watch during restart."
  ],
  "rationale": "Inadequate bracing/kickers for pour pressure is a critical, imminent hazard. Concrete hydrostatic pressure during placement can exceed the capacity of under-braced formwork, leading to sudden blowout or collapse. ACI 347 requires formwork to be designed and constructed to safely support all vertical and lateral loads, including concrete pressure. Because the work is actively in progress, immediate stop-work and engineered corrective action are required.",
  "references": [
    "ACI 347"
  ]
}
